name: PB-09 | Insider Threat — Privileged User Anomaly Detection
description: Production playbook. Ingests privileged account anomaly alerts from Wazuh — off-hours admin logins, lateral spread across systems, bulk sensitive data access, AD modifications, audit log tampering, and unauthorized account creation. Enriches with full AD user context, 30-day behavioral baseline, HR signals, and recent change ticket correlation. Routes to manager notification and account review workflow, or automated account suspension for the highest-confidence threats. Designed specifically for insider threat and privilege abuse scenarios.

trigger.description: Listens for Wazuh privileged account anomaly alerts on rule group: insider_threat,pb09 at level >= 12

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.description: Entry point. Receives Wazuh privileged account anomaly alert payload.

nodes.name: Artifact Extraction & Risk Pre-Classification
nodes.description: Normalizes privileged activity fields. Pre-flags log tampering for zero-tolerance fast-track — clearing the security event log is never a legitimate operation in a monitored enterprise environment.

nodes.name: Log Tampering Zero-Tolerance Gate
nodes.description: Security audit log clearing (EventID 1102) and audit policy modification (4719/4739) bypass the full enrichment pipeline. An insider clearing the security log is actively destroying forensic evidence — this is an immediate account suspension trigger with no score threshold.

nodes.name: Active Directory — Full Privileged User Context
nodes.description: Pulls comprehensive AD user context. Checks all privilege group memberships, account age, recent password changes, logon hours policy, and HR signals stored in extensionAttributes.

nodes.name: Wazuh API — 30-Day Privileged Activity Baseline
nodes.description: Queries Wazuh for the user's privileged activity patterns over 30 days — which systems they typically access, what hours they normally work, how many unique hosts they authenticate to per day. Builds a behavioral baseline to determine whether today's activity is anomalous.

output_variables.baseline_unique_hosts_per_day: {{ $result.data.affected_items | group_by('date') | map('data.win.eventdata.workstationName') | map('unique') | map('length') | average | round(1) }}

nodes.name: Jira — Approved Change Ticket Correlation
nodes.description: Searches Jira change management for an approved, in-progress change ticket assigned to this user covering the current time window. Legitimate admin work during off-hours is always covered by an approved change. No change ticket = unauthorized activity.

params.jql: project = {{ variables.JIRA_CHANGE_PROJECT_KEY }} AND assignee = '{{ $node_02_extract.username }}' AND status in ('Approved', 'In Progress') AND 'Scheduled Start' <= now() AND 'Scheduled End' >= now()

nodes.name: M365 Audit — Recent Admin Operations
nodes.description: Queries M365 Unified Audit Log for the user's admin operations in the last 12 hours — Exchange admin actions, SharePoint permission grants, Azure AD role assignments, Teams policy changes. Builds a complete picture of the user's recent privileged footprint.

params.filter: eventTimestamp ge '{{ now() | subtract_hours(12) }}' and caller eq '{{ $node_02_extract.username }}' and category eq 'Administrative'

output_variables.permission_grants: {{ $result.value | filter(e => e.operationName.value | contains('AppRoleAssignment') or e.operationName.value | contains('DelegatedPermissionGrant')) | length }}
output_variables.policy_changes: {{ $result.value | filter(e => e.operationName.value | contains('Policy') or e.operationName.value | contains('ConditionalAccess')) | length }}
output_variables.high_risk_ops: {{ $result.value | filter(e => e.operationName.value | contains('RoleAssignment') or e.operationName.value | contains('GlobalAdmin') or e.operationName.value | contains('PrivilegedRole')) | map('operationName.value') }}

nodes.name: Insider Threat Risk Score Calculation
nodes.description: Combines all enrichment signals into an insider threat risk score. No approved change ticket for off-hours admin work carries the highest single weight — legitimate admins always have change control coverage. Log tampering, AD modification, and pending termination are additional high-weight signals.

input.formula: ({{ $node_02_extract.is_off_hours }} == true and {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.has_approved_change }} == false ? 30 : 0) + ({{ $node_02_extract.is_ad_modification }} == true ? 20 : 0) + ({{ $node_04_ad_context.is_pending_termination }} == true ? 20 : 0) + ({{ $node_04_ad_context.is_domain_admin }} == true ? 10 : 0) + ({{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.is_new_system_accessed }} == true ? 10 : 0) + ({{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.hosts_today_vs_baseline }} >= 3 ? 15 : {{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.hosts_today_vs_baseline }} >= 2 ? 8 : 0) + ({{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.role_assignments }} > 0 ? 15 : 0) + ({{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.permission_grants }} > 0 ? 10 : 0) + ({{ $node_04_ad_context.is_new_account }} == true ? 15 : 0) + ({{ $node_02_extract.is_account_creation }} == true ? 15 : 0)

nodes.name: Insider Threat Risk Decision Gate
nodes.description: Routes to automated account suspension if score >= 70. Lower-risk signals (off-hours admin with change ticket, new system accessed once) route to manager notification only — less disruptive for legitimate privileged work. Log tampering always triggers suspension via the zero-tolerance gate regardless of score.

nodes.name: Active Directory — Suspend Privileged Account
nodes.description: Disables the privileged AD account. For domain admins and enterprise admins, also removes the account from privileged groups before disabling — prevents the account from being re-enabled and retaining elevated privileges without explicit analyst review.

input.reason: PB-09 | Automated suspension — insider threat risk score: {{ $node_08_score.composite_score }}. Rule: {{ $exec.rule_id }}. Log tamper: {{ $node_02_extract.log_tamper_flag }}

nodes.name: Email — Notify Manager & Security Manager
nodes.description: Sends structured notification email to the user's direct manager and the security manager. Insider threat investigations require manager involvement before any user confrontation — this notification initiates that coordination while preserving investigation confidentiality.

input.subject: [CONFIDENTIAL] Security Alert — Privileged Account Activity Review Required: {{ $node_02_extract.username }}
input.body:
This message is confidential and intended for the recipient and security team only.

Dear {{ $node_04_ad_context.user_manager }},

Our security monitoring system has detected unusual privileged account activity associated with {{ $node_02_extract.username }} ({{ $node_04_ad_context.user_display_name }}) that requires your review.

Activity Summary:
- Alert Type: {{ $exec.rule_name }}
- Timestamp: {{ $exec.timestamp }}
- System Involved: {{ $node_02_extract.hostname }}
- Risk Score: {{ $node_08_score.composite_score }} / 100
- Approved Change Ticket: {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.has_approved_change }} {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.has_approved_change == true ? '(' + $node_06_change_ticket_check.change_ticket_key + ')' : '— NONE FOUND' }}

Required Action:
Please confirm with the Security Operations team whether this activity was authorized and expected.

Do NOT discuss this notification with the employee until contacted by the Security team.

Contact: security@yourorg.com | Priority: HIGH

This is an automated notification from the Security Operations Center.

nodes.name: Azure AD — Revoke Active Sessions
nodes.description: Revokes all active Azure AD sessions for the suspended account. Prevents the insider from maintaining access via existing browser sessions or cached OAuth tokens after account disable.

nodes.name: TheHive — Create Insider Threat Case
nodes.description: Creates a structured TheHive insider threat investigation case. Marked as confidential — insider threat cases require careful handling to avoid tipping off the subject before investigation is complete. Includes full behavioral baseline comparison, change ticket status, M365 admin operations, and investigation checklist.

input.title: PB-09 | Insider Threat — {{ $node_04_ad_context.user_display_name }} ({{ $node_02_extract.username }}) | {{ $exec.rule_name | truncate(50) }} | Score: {{ $node_08_score.composite_score }}
input.description:
## INSIDER THREAT — CONFIDENTIAL

> This case involves a current employee. Do not contact the subject directly. Coordinate with HR and Legal before any confrontation.

**Alert ID:** {{ $exec.alert_id }}
**Timestamp:** {{ $exec.timestamp }}
**Rule:** {{ $exec.rule_name }}
**Risk Score:** {{ $node_08_score.composite_score }} / 100
**Account Suspended:** {{ $node_10_suspend_account.suspension_status }}
**Manager Notified:** {{ $node_11_manager_notify.notification_status }}

### Subject Profile
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Username | {{ $node_02_extract.username }} |
| Display Name | {{ $node_04_ad_context.user_display_name }} |
| Department | {{ $node_04_ad_context.user_department }} |
| Job Title | {{ $node_04_ad_context.user_job_title }} |
| Manager | {{ $node_04_ad_context.user_manager }} |
| Domain Admin | {{ $node_04_ad_context.is_domain_admin }} |
| Enterprise Admin | {{ $node_04_ad_context.is_enterprise_admin }} |
| Account Age | {{ $node_04_ad_context.account_age_days }} days |
| New Account (≤7d) | {{ $node_04_ad_context.is_new_account }} |
| HR Signal | {{ $node_04_ad_context.hr_signal }} |
| Pending Termination | {{ $node_04_ad_context.is_pending_termination }} |

### Triggering Activity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Alert Rule | {{ $exec.rule_name }} |
| Hostname | {{ $node_02_extract.hostname }} |
| Is Off-Hours | {{ $node_02_extract.is_off_hours }} |
| Log Cleared | {{ $node_02_extract.log_cleared }} |
| AD Object Modified | {{ $node_02_extract.ad_object_modified }} |
| AD Object Type | {{ $node_02_extract.ad_object_type }} |
| File Path | {{ $node_02_extract.file_path }} |
| File Access Count | {{ $node_02_extract.file_access_count }} |
| Hosts Accessed | {{ $node_02_extract.hosts_accessed | join(', ') }} |
| Hosts Accessed Count | {{ $node_02_extract.hosts_accessed_count }} |
| New Account Created | {{ $node_02_extract.new_account_name }} |

### Change Management Correlation
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Approved Change Ticket | {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.has_approved_change }} |
| Ticket Key | {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.change_ticket_key | default('None') }} |
| Change Summary | {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.change_ticket_summary | default('N/A') }} |
| Change Scheduled End | {{ $node_06_change_ticket_check.change_scheduled_end | default('N/A') }} |

### Behavioral Baseline Comparison (30 Days)
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Avg Unique Hosts/Day (baseline) | {{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.baseline_unique_hosts_per_day }} |
| Hosts Accessed Today | {{ $node_02_extract.hosts_accessed_count }} |
| Ratio vs Baseline | {{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.hosts_today_vs_baseline | round(1) }}× |
| New System Accessed | {{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.is_new_system_accessed }} |
| Total Auth Events (30d) | {{ $node_05_behavior_baseline.baseline_total_events_30d }} |

### M365 Admin Operations (Last 12 Hours)
| Activity | Count |
|---|---|
| Total Admin Ops | {{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.admin_ops_count }} |
| Role Assignments | {{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.role_assignments }} |
| Permission Grants | {{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.permission_grants }} |
| Policy Changes | {{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.policy_changes }} |
| Mailbox Access | {{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.mailbox_access }} |
| High-Risk Operations | {{ $node_07_m365_recent_activity.high_risk_ops | join(', ') }} |

### Containment Actions Taken
| Action | Status |
|---|---|
| AD Account Suspended | {{ $node_10_suspend_account.suspension_status }} |
| Privileged Groups Removed | {{ $node_10_suspend_account.groups_removed | join(', ') }} |
| Azure AD Sessions Revoked | {{ $node_12_revoke_sessions.revocation_status }} |
| Manager Notification Sent | {{ $node_11_manager_notify.notification_status }} |

### Investigation Checklist — HANDLE WITH CARE
- [ ] Do NOT contact subject until HR and Legal have been briefed
- [ ] Confirm with manager whether activity was authorized
- [ ] Pull full AD audit log for the past 30 days for this account
- [ ] Review all systems accessed ({{ $node_02_extract.hosts_accessed | join(', ') }}) for unauthorized changes
- [ ] Collect M365 audit log export before account re-enable
- [ ] Review all files accessed on sensitive paths — determine if data was copied or exfiltrated
- [ ] Check for new accounts, scheduled tasks, or persistence mechanisms created by this account
- [ ] If log cleared — attempt recovery from Windows event log backup or SIEM
- [ ] Coordinate with HR: if confirmed insider threat, follow HR disciplinary and legal process
- [ ] If account re-enable required — remove from privileged groups, reset password, re-add to standard groups only
- [ ] Document all findings for potential legal proceedings

nodes.name: Slack — Security Manager Alert (Confidential)
nodes.description: Posts to the security manager channel — NOT the general SOC channel. Insider threat investigations are need-to-know only. The SOC channel gets a sanitized high-level alert; the manager channel gets the full detail.

payload.text: *PB-09 | INSIDER THREAT ALERT — CONFIDENTIAL*{{ $node_02_extract.log_tamper_flag == true ? ' :rotating_light: LOG TAMPERING' : '' }}{{ $node_04_ad_context.is_domain_admin == true ? ' — DOMAIN ADMIN' : '' }}

mitre_attack.name: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs

metrics.estimated_mttd_reduction: Real-time — Wazuh fires on privileged event immediately
metrics.estimated_mttr_reduction: Account suspended and sessions revoked in < 5 seconds for log tamper or high-score path
metrics.key_differentiator: Change ticket correlation — no approved change ticket for off-hours admin work = highest single risk weight
